items.summary: The webpage discusses domestic Chinese tech companies' promotion of OpenClaw, a cloud deployment platform, highlighting concerns about their motives—whether for data collection or cloud service sales. The community questions the push for OpenClaw, noting promotional activities like JD.com's giveaway of real lobsters. The discussion reflects skepticism about the platform's technical merits, with no detailed architecture, API documentation, benchmarks, or performance optimization insights provided. Overall, the focus is on the promotional nature and potential privacy or commercial implications rather than technical analysis.

items.title: feat(exec): propagate NO_DNA to child commands · Pull Request #44947 · openclaw/openclaw
highlights:
TypeScript ## feat(exec): propagate NO_DNA to child commands - Author: @beeman
- State: open
- Labels: docs, docker, agents, size: S - Source branch: beeman/no-dna-child-command-propagation
- Target branch: main
- Mergeable: unknown
- Commits: 1 - Additions: 140
- Deletions: 17
- Changed files: 20
- Created: 2026-03-13T09:48:41Z
- Updated: 2026-03-13T17:52:55Z ## Summary - Problem: child processes spawned by OpenClaw did not receive the new proposed standard `NO_DNA` marker, so downstream tools could not reliably detect non-human-operated execution. - Why it matters: tools that honor `NO_DNA` can suppress interactive prompts and other human-targeted UX only when that  ...    - What changed: OpenClaw now injects `NO_DNA=1` with `OPENCLAW_CLI=1` into child command environments for exec, node-host/system.run, sandbox, ACP client, and TUI local shell paths; tests and English docs were updated. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): OpenClaw does not set `NO_DNA` on its own parent process, and login-shell env  ...    - [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra  ...    - [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #N/A
- Related #N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes - Child commands launched by `exec`, node-host/system-run paths, sandbox containers, `openclaw acp client`, and TUI `!`  ...    - Those child commands continue to receive `OPENCLAW_CLI=1`, and path-specific `OPENCLAW_SHELL=*` markers remain intact.  ...    The child-process environment contract changed because supported command runners now export `NO_DNA=1`. The risk is  ...    - How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert this PR/commit, or temporarily remove the child-command `NO_DNA`  ...    https://docs.openclaw.ai/tools/exec
https://docs.openclaw.ai/help/environment
https://docs.openclaw.ai/cli/acp https://docs.openclaw.ai/web/tui ---

### Timeline

**openclaw-barnacle[bot]** added label `docs` · Mar 13, 2026 at 9:48am **openclaw-barnacle[bot]** added label `docker` · Mar 13, 2026 at 9:48am **openclaw-barnacle[bot]** added label `agents` · Mar 13, 2026 at 9:48am **openclaw-barnacle[bot]** added label `size: S` · Mar 13, 2026 at 9:48am **beeman** force-pushed the branch · Mar 13, 2026 at 9:50am **@greptile-apps[bot]** commented · Mar 13, 2026 at 9:51am > Greptile Summary 
> > This PR introduces `NO_DNA=1` propagation into all child-command environments spawned by OpenClaw (exec, node-host, sandbox, ACP client, TUI local shell), while intentionally preserving the parent-process and login-shell probing boundaries where the marker should not appear. The implementation is well-structured: a shared `markOpenClawChildCommandEnv` helper centralizes both `OPENCLAW_CLI=1` and `NO_DNA=1` injection, an opt-out `includeNoDna: false` parameter on `sanitizeHostExecEnv` guards the shell-env probing path, and `ensureOpenClawExecMarkerOnProcess` remains unchanged so the parent OpenClaw process is unaffected.  ...    > 
> Confidence Score: 4/5

items.summary: This pull request addresses a technical issue in OpenClaw where child processes did not inherit the `NO_DNA` marker, which is essential for downstream tools to recognize non-human execution. The update propagates the `NO_DNA=1` environment variable to child commands, enabling tools to suppress interactive prompts and improve automation reliability. This change enhances the robustness and consistency of process handling within OpenClaw, representing a bug fix and a performance improvement in process propagation.

items.summary: The GitHub issue reports a critical bug in OpenClaw version 2026.3.12, where executing basic CLI commands like `gateway status` or `doctor` causes a JavaScript heap out-of-memory (OOM) error, leading to crashes. This problem emerged after upgrading from version 2026.3.11, which previously handled these commands without issues. The crash prevents the CLI from functioning properly, indicating a memory leak introduced in the latest release. The issue is currently open, with no fix yet, and affects the stability and usability of OpenClaw on all supported platforms.

items.summary: The GitHub issue reports a regression in the openclaw project where outbound WhatsApp messaging fails with "No active WhatsApp Web listener," despite auto-replies functioning correctly. Multiple outbound methods—including CLI commands, message tools, and agent sessions—are affected, while inbound and auto-reply features remain operational. The underlying Baileys socket connection is confirmed active, indicating the problem lies in outbound message handling. This issue impacts the reliability of outbound WhatsApp messaging in the platform, requiring further investigation to restore full functionality.

items.summary: This pull request introduces support for the ModelScope API into the openclaw project, enabling users to access community-supported models via an API key. It involves 3 commits with 499 additions across 21 files, and requires users to bind their Alibaba Cloud account to obtain the API key. The update enhances the platform's capabilities, facilitating integration with ModelScope models for improved functionality.

### Summary When using moonshotai/kimi-k2.5 (via NVIDIA NIM, api: openai-completions) as the primary agent in OpenClaw 2026.3.11, the agent completely fails to parse and execute any tools (including read, exec, web_search, etc.). ### Steps to reproduce 1. Configure moonshotai/kimi-k2.5 as the primary model using NVIDIA NIM (integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1) with api:  ...    The agent should successfully parse the tool schema and invoke the tool, as it did reliably in v2026.2.25. Affected: All users using Kimi-k2.5 via NVIDIA NIM with tool use. Severity: High (completely blocks tool use/agent functionality).
Frequency: 100% repro on versions 2026.3.7+. items.summary: The GitHub issue reports a regression in OpenClaw version 2026.3.11, where using the `moonshotai/kimi-k2.5` model via `openai-completions` causes the agent to fail completely in parsing and executing any tools, such as read, execute, or web search. This problem emerged after an update, disrupting tool functionality that previously worked. The issue is reproducible by configuring the specified model with NVIDIA NIM and sending prompts requiring tool execution. The bug significantly impacts workflows relying on tool integrations within OpenClaw.

They display almost instantly (0.1s delay) avoiding the typical OS ~1-2s delay. 2. The browser accessibility is preserved via fallback `aria-label=` for screen readers. ---

items.summary: This pull request introduces a technical update to the openclaw/openclaw WebUI by replacing native `title` attribute tooltips on chat control icons with custom, CSS-based tooltips using `data-tooltip`. This change addresses issues where native tooltips are sluggish or ignored, especially on macOS desktop environments. The update enhances user feedback for controls like Refresh, Thinking Mode, Focus, and Hide Cron by providing immediate, styled tooltips that align with the app's theme, improving usability and responsiveness. The modification involves minimal code changes—adding 81 lines across three files—aimed at performance optimization and a more consistent user experience.

items.summary: This pull request addresses a user experience issue in the openclaw project where skipping authentication during onboarding results in a non-functional agent without clear feedback. To resolve this, the update adds a warning message via `prompter.note()` that informs users when they skip auth, explaining that no API key is configured and guiding them on how to add one later. This enhancement improves transparency and helps prevent confusion or silent failures, without altering the existing ability to skip authentication.

highlights:
Script ## [Bug] v2026.3.11: doctor --fix loops false legacy cron payload kind normalization - Author: @Mdx2025
- State: closed (completed)
- Created: 2026-03-12T07:25:54Z - Updated: 2026-03-13T03:12:37Z
- Closed: 2026-03-13T03:12:37Z
- Closed by: @obviyus ## Summary `openclaw doctor --fix` reports legacy cron payload kind normalization repeatedly on v2026.3.11, even when all jobs  ...    ## Impact - False-positive health warning persists.
- `doctor --fix` appears to "repair" the same jobs on every run.  ...    - OpenClaw: `v2026.3.11`
- Install path: global pnpm package (`openclaw@2026.3.11`) - Gateway: local systemd service, loopback bind  ...    ## Root-cause analysis (local) In the compiled update runner, `normalizePayloadKind(payload)` reports mutation when no effective change is required in  ...    ## Local workaround (confirmed)

Patched local compiled files: - `dist/update-runner-CsfK3SMx.js`
- `dist/update-runner-CvNIx6g2.js`

After patch: - `openclaw doctor --fix` no longer reports legacy kind normalization.  ...    ## Notes Workaround is local and gets overwritten on update/reinstall. A canonical source fix in release artifacts would be ideal. ---

> > That PR landed the root-cause fix in `src/cron/store-migration.ts` so canonical `payload.kind` values no longer trigger false legacy warnings, while whitespace-padded and non-canonical variants still normalize correctly. Closing  ...    **obviyus** closed this · Mar 13, 20
items.summary: The GitHub issue reports that in OpenClaw version v2026.3.11, running `doctor --fix` repeatedly flags a legacy cron payload normalization issue, even when all jobs already have correct `payload.kind` values (`agentTurn` / `systemEvent`). This results in persistent false-positive health warnings and causes the tool to attempt repairs on the same jobs each time, creating unnecessary noise during validation and automation processes. The issue was identified by user @Mdx2025 and was resolved by @obviyus on March 13, 2026.
